topic_title: For loops — repeating an action across a list

length_bias.detail: No question has correct answer as longest by more than 20% of mean. Length bias absent after Q4 reshuffle fix (81 chars vs mean 73, ratio 1.11 ✓).

distractor_plausibility.detail: All distractors are plausible beginner misconceptions: Q1 (wrong index/off-by-one confusion), Q2 (sorted order / reversed order / all-on-one-line), Q3 (1-based counting / inclusive-stop / reversed), Q4 (loop variable naming / += syntax / inline list), Q5 (last-index confusion / max value confusion / len() type restriction), Q6 (wrong initialisation position variants). No absurd distractors.

objective_coverage.note: LO3 (predict output from code) covered by Q2 which requires step-through prediction. All 5 LOs tested.
objective_coverage.detail: All 5 learning objectives covered across 6 questions. No question tests out-of-scope material.

beginner_fairness.detail: All stems in plain language. Code blocks in stems use only 11.6-scope constructs. Every question tests only 11.6 material. Distractors are beginner misconceptions, not advanced-topic trivia.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1). 6-MCQ quiz is clean: positional bias max_share=0.333, no length bias (Q4 corrected), all distractors plausible, all 5 LOs covered. Bias defense applied — positions [2,0,1,3,0,2].
